MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-11-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication as follows:

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff maintains its initial summary judgment burden per Code of Civil Procedure 437c. The court overrules defendant's objections to the Becker declaration. Becker properly lays a foundation regarded the matters attested to and authenticates the business records attached to his declaration, which show that defendant is in default on his credit card account. (See Evid. Code 1400, 1401 [authentication], 1271 [business records exception to hearsay rule]; Unifund CCR, LLC v. Dear (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1 [trial court has broad discretion in admitting business records, bank records are in a different category than normal business records, and a qualified witness need not be the custodian of the business record for it to be admissible].) Plaintiff is entitled to judgment for $5,457.40.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
